link.description:
A four-gigabyte file that won't fit on a USB stick — not because the hardware can't, but because the open format isn't supported, the capable one is patented, and the universal one is from 1996.

Open Nexus is what you build when you decide that's no longer acceptable: a secure, high-performance OS on open hardware, where what a device can do is decided by its hardware and by you. Start with the vision, then see what already runs.
